name: hdd_severe_slow
description: Severely degraded HDD with bursty spikes — sustained ~5–6× slowdown plus occasional 500ms spikes. Models the long-tail (4–5 nines) fail-slow scenario.
sources: FAST 2016 'The Tail at Store': at P99.99 HDDs reach ≥9× slowdown; at P99.999 ≥30×
HotCloud 2013 (limpware): weak-head failures can produce >1s per-I/O latency on affected platters
USENIX ATC 2022: HDD fail-slow events lasting 30–60 min show avg latency 25.7–28.8ms and ratio 2.88–3.75×
notes: spike_ms=500 is a conservative approximation of the 9×/30× tail documented in FAST 2016. Suitable as the primary evaluation scenario for Health-EC tail-latency mitigation. For stress testing only, spike_ms=1000 can be used but should be labeled 'stress test', not 'representative production'.
